data.content:
[plan] Implementation plan for take-loop lease validation before follow-up

## Current behavior (as of dd9c shipped, before this knot)

src/lib/terminal-manager-take-follow-up.ts:sendFollowUpPrompt
unconditionally sends the \"complete or rollback\" follow-up after
turn_ended. It now captures a pre_followup snapshot (foolery-dd9c)
but takes no action based on the lease state.

## Lease state model (from kno lease list --all --json)

- lease_ready    — lease created, not bound to an action step, usable
- lease_active   — bound to an action step in progress, usable
- lease_terminated — terminated (e.g., by kno rollback), unusable

The agent's prompt embeds the lease ID. If we send a follow-up while
the lease is lease_terminated, the agent will run kno claim with a
dead lease and get \"external lease is in state 'lease_terminated'\"
— exactly the maestro-ca91 loop.

## Approach

Insert a lease-state check at the top of sendFollowUpPrompt. If the
lease is no longer in {lease_ready, lease_active}, skip the follow-up
and emit a FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE banner with full identity.

## File-by-file

### Modified

1. src/lib/terminal-manager-take-follow-up.ts
   - Before invoking buildTakeLoopFollowUpPrompt, fetch lease state:
     listLeases(repoPath, true) → find by ctx.entry.knotsLeaseId.
   - If lease is missing OR state is lease_terminated:
     - Emit FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE banner via existing
       emitDispatchFailureBanner machinery. Banner body includes
       sessionId, beatId, currentBeatState, iteration, leaseId,
       leaseState, agentName, agentProvider, agentModel, agentVersion,
       expectedStep, currentStep.
     - Push the banner string into the session UI buffer as a stderr
       event.
     - Emit a lease-audit event lease_dead_on_followup.
     - Record take-loop lifecycle take_loop_follow_up_skipped_dead_lease.
     - Return false (no follow-up sent).
   - If lease is healthy: existing behavior unchanged.

2. src/lib/dispatch-pool-resolver.ts
   - Extend AgentDispatchFailureReason to include 'lease_terminated'
     OR add a new failure kind 'lease_dead' alongside 'agent' and
     'backend' to keep the dispatch-failure schema consistent.
   - Decision: add a new failure kind 'lease_dead' so the existing
     agent-pool failures are not conflated with a runtime lease
     issue. Updates emitDispatchFailureBanner to render the new kind.

### New tests

3. src/lib/__tests__/take-loop-follow-up-dead-lease.test.ts
   - Hermetic. Inject a stub knots backend that returns a single
     lease in state lease_terminated for the bound id. Build a
     minimal TakeLoopContext stub, call sendFollowUpPrompt, assert:
     - returns false
     - no follow-up prompt sent
     - FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE banner emitted with reason=lease_dead
     - take_loop_follow_up_skipped_dead_lease lifecycle recorded
     - lease_dead_on_followup audit event written
   - Second test: lease in lease_ready — assert existing
     follow-up flow is unchanged.

## Reuse

- The dispatch-forensics module (foolery-dd9c) already gives us
  pre_followup snapshots, so this knot's added logging (the dead-lease
  banner + audit) is incremental, not duplicative.
- The DispatchFailureError / emitDispatchFailureBanner /
  buildAnsiRedBanner machinery from dispatch-pool-resolver.ts is
  reused — no new banner-rendering code.

## Test strategy

- Hermetic test injects all deps (no real fs, no real exec).
- Existing follow-up tests in src/lib/__tests__/ cover the healthy
  path; the new test covers the dead-lease branch + the unchanged
  healthy branch as a regression guard.

## Risks

- The lease fetch is async. If it times out or fails, what should
  the follow-up code do? Decision: fail closed — if we cannot
  determine lease state, do NOT send the follow-up and emit a
  FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE banner (reason='lease_state_unknown' or
  similar). This is the conservative choice consistent with CLAUDE.md
  fail-loudly rule. A second failure mode of \"lease list timed out\"
  is preferable to silently looping the agent on a possibly-dead
  lease.

## Acceptance — full match to knot description

- ✅ sendFollowUpPrompt acquires lease state immediately before send.
- ✅ Refuses to send if not lease_ready / lease_active.
- ✅ Banner uses FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE marker.
- ✅ Banner body includes sessionId, beatId, currentBeatState,
  iteration, leaseId, leaseState, leaseTerminatedAt (if available),
  agentName, agentProvider, agentModel, agentVersion, currentStep,
  expectedStep, promptSource, follow-up count for state.
- ✅ Hermetic unit test in src/lib/__tests__/.
- ✅ lease_dead_on_followup lease-audit event.
- Manual repro test for maestro-ca91 path: deferred until we have a
  knots-stub that simulates rollback-then-followup; the hermetic
  test covers the same code path.

## Out of scope (explicit)

- Does NOT address the upstream cause of the rollback (kno
  half-transition vs other) — that is foolery-dd9c's logging job.
- Does NOT change kno semantics around lease termination on
  rollback — that is canonical kno behavior.
- Does NOT auto-recover (e.g., create a new lease and retry) — the
  user explicitly noted in the dd9c discussion that the right
  response depends on root cause, which we still cannot determine.
